prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for acrolein using DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in gas phase via PySCF. Geometry: input XYZ file (8 atoms). Charge/multiplicity: neutral N₀ (0, 1); N₊₁ cation (1, 2); N₋₁ anion (−1, 2) computed for Fukui descriptor generation. Mulliken charge scheme. All SCF calculations converged; charge-conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). 

**Most electrophilic site:** Atom 4 (terminal vinyl carbon, C), f⁺ = 0.6423.
**Most nucleophilic site:** Atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen, O), f⁻ = 0.3939.

Fukui-plus (f⁺) values across all atoms: [0.4014 (O), 0.0223 (C), 0.4340 (C), 0.6423 (C), −0.1573 (H), −0.2072 (H), −0.0621 (H), −0.0734 (H)].

Fukui-minus (f⁻) values: [0.3939 (O), 0.0060 (C), 0.0914 (C), 0.0874 (C), 0.0860 (H), 0.1857 (H), 0.0845 (H), 0.0652 (H)].

Dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻): [0.0075, 0.0163, 0.3426, 0.5549, −0.2432, −0.3929, −0.1466, −0.1386].

The results are chemically consistent with acrolein's known α,β-unsaturated carbonyl reactivity. Visualization saved to agent_call_01_plot.png. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy.
